channel.title: Jornal TRIBUNA DE MACAU EN
channel.description: Jornal TRIBUNA DE MACAU EN RSS

item.title: Young people use AI to solve emotional problems
item.description:
A study carried out by the Sheng Kung Hui Social Services Coordination Office notes that 22.5% of a total of 952 young people in Macau talk to artificial intelligence (AI) to deal with negative emotions. On average, young people interviewed spent 2.3 hours a week using AI tools, however, around 68% were unaware or knew little about “artificial hallucination”. For the association, society must give importance to the needs of young people in this context, and it is important to establish multifaceted emotional support networks
The Macau Sheng Kung Hui Social Services Coordination Office released the results of two surveys carried out last year, which aim to understand “Internet literacy and the current situation of the application of new technologies among young people” and “young people's cognition and healthy behaviors”. In the first survey, 952 local young people were interviewed, aged between 13 and 35. According to this study, 22.5% of respondents talk to artificial intelligence (AI) models to deal with negative emotions, highlighted the association. Among this group of young people, around 35% rated this solution as useful, the association pointed out, noting that, on average, the young people surveyed spent 2.3 hours a week using AI tools, mainly “DeepSeek” and “ChatGPT”, with the purpose of obtaining information, generating content and supporting their studies. However, around 68% of young people interviewed are unaware or know little about “artificial hallucinations”, that is, false, inaccurate or illogical information generated by these AI tools and presented with complete confidence. For the association, this reflects that young people may lack sufficient vigilance regarding the quality and risks of applying results generated by AI. Furthermore, the study indicates that, with high Internet and AI literacy, young people typically have a relatively low level of anxiety and depression. In conclusion, Podemos Ip, director of services at Sheng Kung Hui Social Services Coordinating Office, argued that society should attach importance to young people's needs for emotional support. In his view, young people should, in addition to knowing how to chat online, have more opportunities to be helped by social workers, teachers and family members, as well as practice physical exercise and various leisure experiences, thus establishing multifaceted emotional support networks. Furthermore, according to the same survey, the average daily time that young people spend on social networks increased from 1.64 to 3.53 hours, between 2022, the year in which a similar study was carried out, and 2025. The results of the study also indicate that respondents assessed the level of knowledge about Internet literacy with 7.06 points, on a scale of zero to 10, higher than the 5.71 points given in 2022. However, in terms of practice, young people “obtained relatively low points” with regard to “use and management” and “morality and cognition on the Internet”, pointed out the association. On the other hand, the other study mentioned above, based on valid responses from 932 young people from Macau, also in the 13 to 35 age group, reveals that 65% face sleep quality disorders, with more than 40% “unable to fall asleep within 30 minutes” or “waking up in the middle of the night or early in the morning” one or more times a week. Furthermore, although almost 90% of young people recognize that practicing physical exercise is beneficial for their physical and mental health, only 60% do it weekly, dedicating an average of 3.06 hours per week to physical exercise. Another phenomenon discovered by this survey is that 50% of respondents aged between 13 and 18 usually watch films or read while eating.

item.title: Macau has already surpassed the threshold of 10 million visitors
item.description:
Macao SAR has already received more than 10 million visitors this year, having surpassed the mark on Saturday, 12 days earlier than in 2025, according to data from the Public Security Police Corps. Until the weekend, Macau recorded a daily average of 126 thousand entries, corresponding to a growth of 14% year-on-year. In the first two months of the year, visitors from Mainland China grew by 19%, at the same time as 51% more Portuguese arrived, in annual terms
PEDRO MILHEIRÃO Macau has already surpassed the 10 million visitor mark this year, as announced by the Public Security Police Corps (CPSP). The mark was surpassed on Saturday, 12 days earlier compared to 2025. Of the total visitors, 75.2% came from Mainland China, 15.7% from Hong Kong, 2.5% from Taiwan and 6.6% from abroad. Furthermore, on Saturday, the daily average of visitors was 126 thousand, an increase of 14.2% year-on-year. Between January and February, the Macao SAR registered 7.8 million visitor arrivals, corresponding to a year-on-year increase of 15.1%, according to official data from the Statistics and Census Services Directorate (DSEC). In these two months, international visitors grew 6.8%, to 479 thousand. Nearly six million visitors came from Mainland China, marking an annual increase of 18.9%. Just over 1.1 million visitors arrived from Hong Kong, 0.3% above the number recorded in January and February of last year. At the same time, 182 thousand visitors from Taiwan entered the Macao SAR, 37 thousand more (+26%) on the same comparative basis. The Republic of Korea continues to be the main international market for Macau's tourism industry, taking into account that more than 131 thousand South Korean visitors entered the Macao SAR between January and February, translating into growth of 5%. Looking at the main markets in Asia, 90 thousand visitors arrived from the Philippines, marking an increase of 3.7%. Another 28 thousand entered with a Thai passport, representing an increase of 31.8%. In the same period under analysis, Russia was the country that recorded the greatest growth in year-on-year terms, with more than 11 thousand visitors arriving over the two months, equivalent to an increase of 81.5%. Portugal and Brazil also recorded considerable increases, of 51% and 47.1%, respectively, with 2,144 and 2,280 entries. Comparing arrivals to the territory via entry routes, the 'ferries' brought 452 thousand visitors, in the two months under analysis, 3.5% less than the number recorded in the same period of 2025. However, 6.5 million tourists arrived by land, a year-on-year increase of 17.6%. More than 1.2 million visitors entered the Hengqin Border Post, up 36% year on year. By air transport, 578 thousand tourists arrived, a year-on-year increase of 14.2%. Analyzing entries according to length of stay, the 4.9 million day-trippers who entered Macau in January and February represent 63% of the total, compared to the 2.8 million tourists who stayed overnight in the Macao SAR. In annual terms, excursionists and tourists grew by 22% and 5%, respectively. According to DSEC, the average accumulated period of stay for visitors was one day, while tourists stayed 2.2 days in the territory, on average, similar to the first two months of 2025.
New Year's Eve Exhibitions and Parades attracted around 640 thousand people The two Year of the Horse Celebration Parades that took place near Lake Sai Van and in the northern part of the city were attended by around 85 thousand visitors, according to a report from the Directorate of Tourism Services (DST). Together with the exhibitions of the 17 floats at Fisherman's Wharf, the activities attracted approximately 640 thousand visitors, having enriched “the visual experience of the festivities”, “the festive atmosphere and the tourist attractiveness of the city's community areas”, says DST. The organization also estimated that more than 270,000 spectators watched the three drone and fireworks shows live. Preliminary data relating to the Giant Golden Dragon Parade indicate that around 140 thousand people participated in the 20 shows that took place in various areas of the city. DST's online games, as part of the festivities, registered more than one million participants.

item.title: State Defense Commission wants to protect Chinese culture
item.description:
The spokesperson for the Executive Council, Wong Sio Chak, said that the State Security Defense Commission will also serve to protect Chinese culture, stating that, “to destroy a country it is not necessary to do so through military means or the internet”. The Secretary for Administration and Justice also argued that “the destruction of culture can be more invisible”, which is why the integration of the president of the Cultural Institute and the director of Education Services into this Commission is justified. On the other hand, he indicated that the CDSE Secretariat will have 34 workers and five departments
Catarina Pereira
The strengthening of national security in the territory also aims to protect Chinese culture, which is why the Commission for the Defense of State Security (CDSE) now includes the president of the Cultural Institute (IC) and even the director of the Directorate of Education and Youth Development Services (DSEDJ), explained the spokesperson for the Executive Council. "We have to guarantee the security of culture. Nowadays, to destroy a country it does not have to be through military means or the internet, it can be through a cultural invasion, cultural intervention or cultural elimination", said Wong Sio Chak in the presentation of the administrative regulations relating to the organization and functioning of the CDSE Secretariat. The Secretary for Administration and Justice also added that “the destruction of culture can be more invisible”, so the IC and DSEDJ must play an “important role” in defending the security of the State. “There will be a lot of work in terms of publicizing the importance of Chinese culture and the Secretary will also give instructions to these public services to better promote this work”, said Wong Sio Chak. The administrative regulations relating to the CDSE Secretariat were presented one day after the Commission's bill was unanimously approved in the Legislative Assembly. This law establishes that lawyers who want to represent defendants accused of violating the Law on the Defense of State Security must obtain a special authorization, after providing information regarding family members, nationality and connection with organizations or individuals abroad and others. There is no right to appeal from the binding opinion of the Commission, which is chaired by the Chief Executive. However, it should be noted that the law does not present the criteria for a lawyer to be accepted or not by the CDSE. Furthermore, under the law, the judge may decide to hold the trial behind closed doors, taking into account possible harm to the interests of State security. A decision that must also be validated by the Commission. According to the administrative regulations, the Secretariat reports to the president of the CDSE, functioning, in the administrative structure, “directly dependent on the Chief Executive”, but enjoying “administrative autonomy”.
Three dozen employees and five departments The CDSE Secretariat will consist of five departments and a total of 34 employees. Only the appointments of managers will be published in the Official Bulletin, including the deputy general secretary, a role that will be assumed by the director of the Judiciary Police. As Wong Sio Chak explained, the hiring of the remaining employees – which will be “very strict” – will not be disclosed. The Commission's departments include the Department of Policy Studies, Risk Prevention and Control, Liaison and Coordination, Outreach and Education and General Support. In accordance with the regulations, the files and processes of the previous Office of the Macao SAR State Security Defense Commission will be transferred to the Secretariat. Furthermore, the responsibilities of the Judicial Police Security Department that coincide with those of the Secretariat are eliminated and, as a result, the General Division of Matters relating to State Security is abolished.

item.title: Lunar New Year left inflation at highest level since 2024
item.description:
The accumulated inflation rate in the Macao SAR rose to 0.44% in February, reaching the highest level in two years, due to the impact of the Chinese New Year, according to official statistical data
The general average consumer price index (IPC) in Macau grew 0.44% in the annual cycle ending in February compared to the 12 immediately previous months (between March 2024 and February 2025), reaching the highest level in two years and surpassing the 0.33% recorded both in January 2026 and in the general calculation of the previous year, announced the Statistics and Census Service (DSEC). The organization associates the increase with the fact that the Lunar New Year took place this year in February, while in 2025 it was celebrated in January. Considering the same comparative basis, the most used to calculate the annual inflation rate, the IPC grew 0.39% in bracket A, which includes around half of households with average monthly expenses between 11,000 and 35,999 patacas, and 0.48% in bracket B, which covers 30% of families whose average expenses vary between 36,000 and 71,999 patacas. On the other hand, the general CPI rose 1.16% compared to February 2025, also marking the biggest increase in two years. According to DSEC, the price index for the leisure, recreation, sports and culture section (+7.76%) experienced the largest increase year-on-year, due to the increase in the price of hotel rooms and excursions during the Lunar New Year period. The upward trend extended to food products and non-alcoholic drinks, whose index increased 0.92%, compared to February 2025, mainly due to the prices of meals purchased outside the home and take-away. The price index for the housing and fuel section increased by 0.37%, due to the rise in house rents. The annual increases also involved miscellaneous products and services (+3.04%), which include personal care, insurance, jewelry, goldsmithing and watches, as well as the transport category (+2.36%). Compared to January 2026, the general CPI increased by 0.42%, again with the emphasis on the leisure, recreation, sport and culture section (+6.05%). The price indices for transport (+1.63%), miscellaneous products and services (+0.63%) and food products and non-alcoholic beverages (+0.22%) also registered monthly increases, countering the declines in alcoholic beverages and tobacco (-0.96%) and clothing and footwear (-0.58%). DSEC data also indicates that the average general CPI grew by 0.85% in the first two months of 2026, compared to the same period last year.
S.T.

item.title: Gastronomy Festival shares experiences between Creative Cities
item.description:
Until the 29th of this month, Doca dos Pescadores hosts 38 UNESCO Creative Cities. Music, cinema, literature, crafts and popular arts, multimedia and design will also be highlighted at the “International Gastronomy Festival”. The event will also take place at ZAPE, with the aim of promoting economic vitality in the area. Included in the program, a gastronomic Forum will take place today, with the presence of academics, top international content creators and representatives of the restaurant sector
VÍTOR REBELO
Fans of diverse cuisine, representing various countries or regions around the world, have another seven days, until March 29, to visit the stands of the “International Festival of Gastronomic Cities, Macau 2026”. Authorities expect 100,000 people to visit the event. The main location is Praça do Coliseu Romano and Legend Boulevard da Doca dos Pescadores, with a secondary space being created for the first time located on Rua de Cantão and Rua de Shanghai, in ZAPE, with a view to boosting the economy of the community neighborhood. At Doca dos Pescadores, the “International Gastronomy Avenue” brings together 100 stalls with culinary delicacies and will offer 53 “Gastronomy Cities Demonstrations”. To this end, the organization, under the responsibility of the Directorate of Tourism Services (DST), invited famous chefs to present their skills. Among them are two young people working in Macau and one from South Africa, selected by the Young Chefs Program, an initiative of the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO). Together, a “World Wine Lounge”, launched in this edition, will be available, which presents wines, beers, whiskeys and spirits. The place has around 800 seats for meals, with cultural and artistic shows, interactive programs and themed installations for taking photographs, “in order to create a lively and festive atmosphere”, according to DST. In the secondary enclosure, visitors can find the “International Market for Excellent Gastronomic Products”, which features a total of 40 stands from 16 Creative Cities of Gastronomy, including Macau, and five Creative Cities from other areas, offering a wide range of food and related products. The stalls will be dedicated to the theme “Typical Macau Brand” and “Café”, where there will be exhibitions, “Demonstrations of the Art of Drinks” and interactive shows. Enquanto isto, hoje decorrerá o “Fórum Internacional de Gastronomia, Macau” sob o tema “Fusão Criativa: Reimaginando a Gastronomia para Além do Prato”. Representantes da Rede de Cidades Criativas da UNESCO, da FAO, criadores internacionais de conteúdos de topo, representantes do sector da restauração e académicos partilharão as suas ideias. With the Hong Kong newspaper South China Morning Post as a partner, the seminar includes three lectures and three parallel thematic discussions between 9:30 am and 12:30 pm. A group of speakers will explore how intersectoral cooperation in the area of ​​gastronomy can boost the development of a creative city, combining stories, experiences and innovation. Invited to the forum were the project manager for the UNESCO Creative Cities Network Programme, Qiaobo Ni, the head of the Youth Culture and Food Heritage Program of the FAO Youth and Women's Office, Flora Igoe, the famous content creators of the “Korean Englishman” and “Jolly” channels on YouTube, Josh Carrott and Ollie Kendal, who will define the contours of the main themes under discussion. A Festa Gastronómica apresenta também a primeira edição do “Chinese Creative Showcase”, convidando sete cidades de diferentes áreas criativas do Interior da China para expor a sua cultura distintiva. One of the highlights of the festival will be the presence of UN Tourism Ambassador Khaby Lame, leader in the number of Tik Tok followers worldwide (160 million), who will promote a series of in-depth travel experiences.
“Much more than a culinary celebration” At the opening ceremony, presided over by the Secretary for Economy and Finance, Tai Kin Ip, the director of DST said that the number of cities participating in this edition reached a new record, which demonstrates the “attraction and appeal of Macau as a platform for international exchange”. Maria Helena de Senna Fernandes also said that the territory will continue to develop “its unique advantages of meeting Chinese and Western cultures, to deepen exchange and cooperation with Mainland China and the various Creative Cities of Gastronomy worldwide”. In this way, he added, Macau promotes “transmission, innovation and exchange” between the Creative Cities of Gastronomy, so that it becomes “a global language for promoting innovation and sustainable development”. For her part, Denise Bax, secretary of the UNESCO Creative Cities Network, stated that “thanks to the passion of its organizers and participants, together with the involvement of local communities and international partners, the festival has become much more than a culinary celebration, constituting a vibrant space where cities exchange ideas, share experiences and start new conversations in diverse local contexts”. During the event, various promotional and experience activities will be launched, including immediate discount on consumption through electronic payment, sweepstakes, limited themed games at the main location, limited free coffee experience at the secondary location, among others. Consumption tickets can be overlapped to enjoy various benefits. On the other hand, ZAPE establishments also offer limited-time discounts. Participants can collect a themed card free of charge and present it at designated commercial establishments.

item.title: Continuous improvement optimizes subsidy rules
item.description:
The Government has updated the regulations for access to subsidies granted under the Continuous Development and Improvement Program", publishing new rules for the next four years. Financial support remains at 6,000 patacas, but beneficiaries will not need to present proof of payment of course fees to the Education Services when submitting the application for granting the subsidy
VÍTOR REBELO
The “Continuous development and improvement program for the years 2026 to 2029” will continue to grant a subsidy of 6,000 patacas, applied to higher education and continuing education courses and accreditation exams organized by local and foreign institutions that take place between July 1st of this year and June 30th, 2029. The main changes to the new phase of the plan, defined after discussion in the Executive Council (EC), come into force on April 1, 2026, and concern the exemption from presenting proof of payment of course fees to the Directorate of Education and Youth Development Services (DSEDJ) when applying for a subsidy. On the other hand, due to the interconnection and interconnection of data, if DSEDJ can obtain the learning license and proof of presence of the beneficiaries in the driving test corresponding to the course attended, issued by the Directorate of Services for Traffic Affairs (DSAT), the respective request does not require instruction with such documents. Furthermore, the Government will optimize the way the subsidy is awarded and extend the use of the model based on “pay first and submit the subsidy request later” to local continuing education courses. This means that trainees must pay their tuition fees in advance at the time of registration and, if the course attendance rate reaches 70%, they will be able to request support through an electronic application. However, there are some requirements: when the beneficiary enrolls in a course whose amount of subsidy to be used is equal to or greater than 3,000 patacas; the attendance rate is less than 30% in any of the courses for which the subsidy is used; the beneficiary's overall presence rate in the previous program (between 2023 and 2026) is less than 30%; or if the beneficiary has not fulfilled specific duties. At the same time, the amount of the deposit will be increased to an amount equivalent to 50% of the corresponding tuition fees, while maintaining the provision that the refund of the deposit will only take place when the beneficiary's attendance rate reaches 70%. The EC statement also indicates that, “to regulate the scope of application to local institutions, the ‘Program from 2026 to 2029’ expressly accepts the participation of public services and entities and higher education institutions, private educational institutions of non-higher education, public administrative utility associations, unions, trade union federations, social facilities and driving schools legally constituted in the Macao SAR”. Institutions that currently participate in the plan may continue to enjoy the benefits as long as they meet the requirements defined by this administrative regulation, and will not be affected by the new provisions. To optimize the rules for using financial support in the “Program from 2026 to 2029”, a provision will be added that restricts the registration of heads of local institutions, their partners or administrators or the main heads of their bodies in continuing education courses organized by these local institutions. “To ensure a harmonious transition between the Programs, institutions authorized to participate can submit requests for the organization of continuing education courses during the first regular period for submission of requests, in April 2026”, highlights the note, adding that the program will continue to be articulated with the training measures for qualified staff adopted by the Macao SAR in different areas and the Lines of Government Action that “prioritize diversified learning needs”. It is worth remembering that the Government launched between 2011 and 2026 five phases of the “Continuous Development and Improvement Program”, with the aim of supporting residents’ permanent learning. So far, the plan has had more than 1.56 million participations.

item.title: Institutions went to Vienna to discuss the fight against drugs
item.description:
Included in the Chinese delegation, Macau had 14 members of non-governmental institutions and youth associations in another session of the UN Commission on Narcotic Drugs. At the Vienna meeting, members of the Macao SAR stated that, among other measures, the territory will monitor the legislative work regarding the inclusion of new substances in the list subject to control
The 69th session of the United Nations (UN) Commission on Narcotic Drugs took place in Vienna, Austria, with the presence of representatives from Macau, belonging to non-governmental institutions, youth associations and universities, who participated in the meeting and parallel activities. Some of the members of the territory's delegation, part of the Chinese delegation, gave speeches, noting that the Macao SAR will continue to coordinate with the work to combat drugs carried out at national level and comply with the obligations of international conventions. Furthermore, according to a statement from the Institute of Social Action (IAS), the territory's institutions will monitor Macau's legislative work regarding the inclusion of new substances, comply with the obligations of international conventions and promote the active role of non-governmental organizations “to help young people make their efforts in the development of the country and the Macao SAR”. Representing the IAS, the vice-president, Hoi Va Pou, and the head of department, Cheang Io Tat, were present. From the Judicial Police, the deputy director, Sou Sio Keong, and the functional head, Lei Hon Nei, moved. The opening ceremony of the event was attended by more than 2,000 people, from more than 110 member countries, international organizations and non-governmental organizations, in which a discussion took place on international drug control and the implementation of respective policies. Among the Macao SAR's non-governmental institutions, the Macau Drug Addict Rehabilitation Association was one of those present, represented by its president, Augusto Nogueira. The work agenda included the implementation status of international drug control treaties, the assessment and approval of the inclusion of three substances on the list subject to control and the implementation of actions following the “Ministerial Declaration of 2019”. Other issues were also addressed, such as cooperation and coordination between institutions for the treatment and response to global drug problems, as well as the implementation, evaluation and execution of the “2030 Agenda on sustainable development”, highlights the IAS note. At the meeting, Chinese representatives, led by the deputy secretary-general of the National Narcotics Control Commission, Gao Wei, participated in the general debate and reiterated the approach to the issue of synthetic drugs, through a strategy of global balance between reducing demand and supply and combating the legalization of drugs. At the same time, “international society was called upon to achieve real multilateralism, firmly defend the authority of the three anti-drug conventions and the current drug control system, continuously reinforce cooperation at international level in responding to drug problems”, says the IAS text. The session featured more than 160 parallel meetings and exhibitions.
V.R.

item.title: Low birth rate requires “radical measures”
item.description:
Researchers told Lusa that, to reverse the lowest birth rate in the world, Macau needs “more radical measures” than mere subsidies to improve the lives of the entire population. In 2025, the Macao SAR registered 2,871 newborns, the lowest number in almost half a century. In the previous year, the fertility rate had been 0.58 births per woman, far from what was needed to replace generations (2.1). According to estimates by the United Nations Department of Economic and Social Affairs, Macau will have the lowest birth rate in the world in 2024, followed by Singapore, with a fertility rate of 0.95 births per woman. Something that “reflects structural pressures common in many urban economies in East Asia where the cost of living is high”, said Emma Zang Xiaolu, a sociologist at Yale University, in the USA. In Hong Kong, 15 primary schools are at risk of closing after the region recorded around 31,100 births in 2025, the lowest number ever. Mainland China recorded 7.92 million births in 2025, the lowest figure since the founding of the People's Republic of China in 1949. The birth rate has also fallen to historic lows. “Those very conventional life trajectories, about the age at which you should get married and, after you get married, you should have children, those norms have been weakening,” said Mu Zheng, a sociologist at the National University of Singapore. As East Asia has become more developed and wealthier, people "value individual preferences more," although the influence of "family and family values" is even greater than in the West, he added. But family pressure collides with “many other types of pressures”, Mu Zheng highlighted, including expectations of good academic performance and, later, a “very demanding” work culture. Emma Zang also points out “long working hours” as factors that make it difficult to start a family, along with “housing costs and uncertainty about future economic opportunities”. Macau was the 13th most expensive city in the world in 2024, according to a survey by the North American consultancy firm Mercer, mainly due to housing prices. The pressures felt in other East Asian cities “are intensified” in Macau, highlighted Emma Zang, “by a highly specialized economy and limited housing space”. Having children is not yet in Emily Cheong's plans, 29 years old, despite having been married for three years. “We are still living with my parents, trying to save for a down payment on an apartment”, explained the resident to Lusa. The couple's situation became complicated last year, when the 'satellite casino' where Cheong's husband worked, a 'croupier' who managed to find a new job, but with a lower salary, closed. The Macau Government introduced measures to encourage birth rates, such as a subsidy, worth a total of 54 thousand patacas, for children up to three years old. But Emma Zang is not optimistic: “Government incentives may help marginally, but global studies suggest that financial subsidies alone rarely reverse the decline.” “Many of these policies are not particularly effective,” confirmed Mu Zheng, because the incentives “may not be large enough to really remove all the anxiety” from young adults. The sociologist gave as an example the expectation, placed on parents, of “an intensive investment of time, energy and money in the development and education of their children”. Emma Zang believes that “improved housing affordability, work-family balance, and childcare availability tend to be more important for long-term demographic stabilization.” The Macau Government promised to offer, free of charge, more and better daycare centers and launched a public consultation, which ended on Monday, on the increase, in the private sector, of maternity leave, from 70 to 90 days. Policies that “really support young families and encourage gender equality”, particularly in the division of domestic labor, can work, but Mu Zheng emphasizes that even in Scandinavia the effect has faded. The sociologist says that, in the long term, “more radical and comprehensive measures” are needed, including “creating a work culture that is more friendly” to family life and “reducing the pressure on mental health”.
JTM with Lusa

item.title: Reorganization of the DSF “strengthens” law enforcement
item.description: As part of the Public Administration reform, the Tax Execution Center replaced the Tax Execution Division of the Financial Services Directorate (DSF), according to a press release from the Executive Council (EC). The new DSF department is “responsible for carrying out tax enforcement functions” enshrined in the Tax Code, “thus strengthening the specialty and uniformity in the execution of the law”, can be read in the EC note. Aiming for greater government efficiency, the new regulation on the organization and functioning of the Financial Services Directorate defines that the organization's structure changed from six to five departments and from 13 to 11 divisions, in addition to a dependent body, after the extinction of three sectors and two sections. In addition to the Tax Execution Center and according to the administrative regulations now in force, the Tax Administration Department includes the Tax Services Division and the Tax Collection Division. The Tax Inspection and External Fiscal Affairs Department comprises the Tax Inspection Division and the External Fiscal Affairs Division. The Budget Management Division and the Public Expenditure and Treasury Management Division are part of the Public Accounting Department. In turn, the Asset Management Department includes the Real Estate Management Division and the Movable Asset Management and Asset Inventory Division, while the Smart Construction Division is part of the Planning and Financial Intelligence Department. Finally, the Legal and Notary Division and the Financial Administrative Division complete the structure of the DSF, which remains headed by a director, assisted by two deputy directors. Quoted by Radio Macau, Wong Sio Chak, spokesperson for the EC, assured that no employee will be fired or lose benefits.

item.title: Exploring Hengqin “is really to fulfill” the mission of diversifying the economy
item.description:
Macau and Guangdong achieved “remarkable new results” in cooperation in relevant sectors, namely economy and trade, finance, science and technology, population well-being, education, health and social security, according to the Chief Executive. At the Guangdong Spring reception, Sam Hoi Fai also highlighted that Macau will take advantage of opportunities and take advantage of the momentum of the national Five-Year Plan to deepen and consolidate cooperation with the neighboring province, as well as continue to invest in Hengqin as a path for economic diversification
VÍTOR REBELO
The Chief Executive stated that over the past year, under the guidance and support of the central authorities and through the joint efforts of Guangdong and Macau, cooperation between the two parties has produced “fruitful results”. Speaking at the Guangdong Province Spring 2026 reception in Macau, Sam Hou Fai stressed that the Government should “proactively align with national development strategies and seize opportunities and momentum to deepen and consolidate cooperation”. Recalling that, for the first time, Guangdong, Hong Kong and Macau co-organized an edition of the National Games and the Special Olympics Games for the Disabled, the Government official indicated that exchanges and cooperation between the two sides “have become more frequent at all levels and in all areas and the two-way flow of people between Guangdong and Macau has become increasingly dynamic”. Sam Hou said that the period of the 15th Five-Year Plan for National Economic and Social Development of the People's Republic of China represents “a crucial phase in consolidating the foundations and taking comprehensive steps towards realizing China's socialist modernization”, also bringing “greater opportunities and the creation of a broader space in which it becomes possible to deepen cooperation between Guangdong and Macau” and raise it to “new heights and new levels”. Considering that Guangdong is one of China's economic engines, maintaining its position as the country's leader for 37 consecutive years, the Chief Executive said that the two-way flow of people between the two sides has become increasingly dynamic. Furthermore, he highlighted that new progress was also achieved in the development of the Greater Bay Area and the facilitation of border crossings was further strengthened. “Guangdong and Macau have achieved remarkable new results in cooperation in relevant sectors, namely economy and trade, finance, science and technology, population well-being, education, health and social security”, he highlighted. Elsewhere in his speech, Sam Hou Fai expressed that the high-quality development of the Hengqin Cooperation Zone constitutes the “crucial task” for cooperation between Guangdong and Macau under the new circumstances. “As joint coordinators of the Cooperation Zone Management Committee, we are fully aware that the original intention of exploring Hengqin is to fulfill the fundamental mission of promoting the adequate diversification of Macau’s economy”, emphasized Sam Hou Fai. Furthermore, he continued, the two parties “must consistently adhere to the strategic positioning of ‘Macau + Hengqin’, align with the reference criteria and operational requirements of the ‘three checks’ and ‘three connectivities’, jointly implement the strategic guidelines of the Central Government, firmly reform, pool efforts and overcome challenges, to leverage a new situation in the development of the Hengqin Cooperation Zone”. Sam Hou Fai hopes that Guangdong will focus on priority sectors, “so that distinct industries with their own characteristics can be developed and the synergistic effect of industry clusters can be enhanced”. At the same time, he wants “a new and greater impetus to be injected into the integrated development of Macau and Hengqin, as well as the integration of the Greater Bay Area market”, through the creation of a “portfolio of emblematic ventures and projects with a significant multiplier effect”.

item.title: DST expects Central Asia to compensate for drop in Western visitors
item.description:
The war in the Middle East will have an impact on Macau's tourism industry, acknowledged the director of Tourism Services, during the launching ceremony of the International Cities Gastronomy Festival. Maria Helena de Senna Fernandes designed a new strategy, aimed at the Central Asian market, in order to compensate for a potential drop in Western tourists
As part of the inauguration of the International Festival of Cities of Gastronomy, Maria Helena de Senna Fernandes recognized that the ongoing war in the Middle East will have an impact on the arrival of Western tourists, especially from Europe. The director of the Tourist Office (DST) assured the Chinese-language press that authorities will pay attention to the possible impacts of the war. Furthermore, he said that the “Arabian Travel Market” exhibition, scheduled for May, will be postponed until August this year. However, she stated that she was not “scared” by the expected impact and highlighted the need to rethink the strategy. The director said she believes that people will prefer to travel within Asia, to the detriment of long-haul trips, adding that Asian visitors could compensate for a possible drop in Western tourists. In this sense, he said that the Macao SAR will continue to focus on the international tourist market and that it will resort to cooperation with the Ministry of Culture and Tourism of China in Central Asia. In order to explore the market in this region, DST plans to invite tourism entrepreneurs from Central Asia to visit Macau and discuss forms of cooperation. In relation to the European market, DST will also launch online learning resources, in English and German, aimed at travel agencies. Helena de Senna Fernandes also recalled that negotiations have already been held with travel agency associations in Germany about future cooperation. In this context, DST intends to cooperate with more European travel agency associations and plans to strengthen cooperation with cultural centers and tourist representations of the Chinese Ministry of Culture and Tourism in Europe. For Andy Wu, president of the Macau Tourism Industry Association, the Macao SAR is expected to receive 44 million tourists this year, four million more (+10%) than those expected in 2025. For the association leader, Macau needs to improve its infrastructure and, in particular, transport, with the creation of new careers on public holidays, in order to respond to a greater influx of visitors. Andy Wu also suggested holding events related to international football, such as friendly games between renowned teams, in order to offer a more diversified tourist offer to tourists from the interior of the country.
P.M.

item.title: Mulan Theater ends season of 37 Chinese shows
item.description:
The Mulan theater play will be the last event, of a total of 37 scheduled since November for the first season of Chinese shows promoted by the Cultural Institute. The performance belongs to the Ningbo Arts Group
The series of events that made up the first season of “Chinese Culture Shows” will end with the theater play Mulan, to be held over two days, March 27th and 28th, Friday and Saturday, at 7:30 pm, in the Grand Auditorium of the Cultural Center. Jointly directed by Zhou Liya and Han Zhen, acclaimed as the “twin stars of Chinese dance”, the show will be led by the Ningbo Arts and Entertainment Group. At the same time, several workshops and complementary activities will be promoted. Mulan is a classic legend well known in China. Centered on filial piety, loyalty, courage and love, the work narrates the journey and transformation of Mulan, from a beautiful young peasant girl into a legendary heroine, “thus transmitting traditional Chinese virtues and humanist feelings that transcend time and space”, highlights the Cultural Institute (IC). By “ingeniously” fusing dance, martial arts, theater and technological elements, the production gives “renewed vitality to classic characters”, he adds. The work was honored with several awards, including the prestigious “Chinese Dance Lotus Flower”, having already toured several countries on a world tour. The Ningbo Arts and Entertainment Group has won several national awards, including the “Five-One Project” and the “Chinese Dance Lotus Flower”, with performances in various countries and regions, both in China and abroad. In conjunction with the Mulan presentations, the organization will promote artistic experience workshops, namely quilling (art in rolled paper) and light clay modeling, aimed at participants aged six and over, which will take place on the 15th, 22nd and 29th of March, from 3pm to 5pm at Casa Lou Kau. There will also be the “Dress and Dance like Mulan” workshop, which presents the experience of wearing ethnic Chinese dance costumes for parents and children, for children aged four to eight, on March 14, with two sessions, from 10:30 am to 12:30 pm and from 3:00 pm to 5:00 pm. Another extra event, “Filigree on Paper”, will be held on the 14th, 21st and 28th, between 3pm and 5pm. Under the sponsorship of the Secretariat for Social Affairs and Culture, organized by the IC and co-organized by the Department of Publicity, Culture and Sports of the Liaison Office of the Central Government in the Macao SAR and with the support of the Tourism Services, Education and Youth Development Services and the Institute for Municipal Affairs, the “1st Season of Chinese Culture Shows” has presented, since November, 37 quality shows, exchanges and extra activities, seen by more than 10 thousand people.
V.R.

item.title: IC promotes publicity actions on the installation of claims
item.description: To reinforce society's awareness of safeguarding cultural heritage and, in particular, deepen the knowledge of residents, traders and professionals about the rules for installing signs and signs in protection zones, the Cultural Institute (IC) promoted several legal dissemination actions. In this context, it also installed a publicity post in Largo de S. Domingos, “aiming to reinforce legal awareness in the area of ​​heritage and foster a sense of shared responsibility in the community”. Stressing that it has been committed to promoting knowledge on this subject, the organization led by Leong Wai Man stressed that, this year, the focus was on local salespeople and professionals. Thus, leaflets were distributed in several locations in the central area, including Avenida de Almeida Ribeiro, Largo do Senado, Largo de S. Domingos, Rua de São Paulo and Rua da Felicidade, alerting traders to the obligation to obtain licenses in advance before installing any signs and signs. During these visits, “the technical requirements of the instructions were presented in a clear and accessible way, such as the scope of installation, location, quantity, type and dimensions, as well as the prohibition on the use of bright colors”. At the station in Largo de S. Domingos, information panels were installed, as well as promotional materials distributed. At the same time, clarifications were provided by IC staff, and the public “was able to find out about the requirements and precautions to be taken when installing, maintaining and repairing signs and signs, as well as about carrying out works in classified properties and respective protection zones”. The IC states that it will continue to promote work to publicize the Law on Safeguarding Cultural Heritage, aiming to deepen social awareness for the protection of heritage and preserve the city's historical resources.

item.title: Danish politicians look for voters even in the sauna
item.description:
During election periods, Danes usually receive cookies and flowers from candidates, but it is less common to find them half-naked in saunas, as happens during the campaign for the legislative elections on March 24th. “It's a little different, because people are relaxed and we're closed in there, so we're not leaving,” explained smiling Gitte Droger, a 53-year-old voter who often frequents the sauna at a recreation center in Copenhagen's Vanlose neighborhood. Two candidates from Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen's Social Democratic Party met voters for a sweat session, dressed in their swimwear. MP Ida Auken and Justice Minister Peter Hummelgaard came together to talk to 18 voters about issues such as climate, energy, inequality and crime, while throwing water on the hot stones in the sauna. The heat was intense and enveloping, while the audience – also in swimsuits, sitting on towels on wooden benches – listened to Auken and Hummelgaard present their ideas and answer questions. “It’s the first time I’ve campaigned in a sauna,” Auken told AFP. “This entire election campaign represented a lot of new things for me, different campaign activities: playing handball or going to bars instead of debating at round tables”, he stated. Hummelgaard even said that he tried, for the first time, an ice bath between two sauna sessions. “The campaign also has to be fun,” he said, with a smile. The Social Democrats, in power since 2019 and at the head of a coalition of left and right, lead public opinion polls with around 21.5% of the votes, although this percentage is six points lower than that obtained in the 2022 elections. As is normal, post-election negotiations are expected to determine the form adopted by the next government, although it may take some time, which has already happened. In this Scandinavian country, having easy access to elected representatives is almost a given. “It's important for people to see us in a different way, so they can see that we are real human beings, see what moves us and regain some confidence,” said Ida Auken, who is running for a sixth term in the Danish Parliament. Sauna visitors did not go through any control system before meeting with politicians, and there was no security at the event. Seeing politicians in the sauna is not unimaginable in the country, said voter Miriam Hvidt. “In Denmark, it is not at all strange to see a minister diving into ice water”, he said, adding that it is common to meet politicians in the supermarket or at the cinema. “I can't imagine that, in a big country like the United States, it is common to see a high-ranking official mixing with the population,” said Hvidt.
JTM with international agencies
